title: Post detail
surface: Single-post read with v2 voice register
summary: v1's reading experience carries forward (per brownfield diagnose: post-detail reflows cleanly, narrow column reads well). v2 changes: voice register delta (D5), end-of-post next-step affordance, footer signature.

strategy.decision: Preserve v1's reading column, callouts, and pullquote behavior. Change the post's frame: byline + format-badge + read-time + (optional Dispatch number per D1) lead the post; footer carries one next-step (related, series-next, or counterpoint).
strategy.why: The brownfield diagnose found post-detail to be the most intact surface in v1. Per Rule 1 (match the intended brand register), the reading experience itself is one of v1's strongest assets — preserving it is correct. What changes is the framing around the read (byline placement, format badge, end-of-post next-step), not the read itself.
strategy.shipped: v1 post detail surfaces title + date + tags at top, MDX body with callouts and pullquotes, related-posts component at footer. Working well per current-state captures.
strategy.gap: v2 post detail adds: (a) format badge (Post / Whitepaper / Counterpoint) for cross-format consistency; (b) optional Dispatch # if Thesis B; (c) one next-step at footer (not three competing choices); (d) author block above the fold per home-page consistency.
strategy.question: Does the voice register evolve from the v1 guide (Thesis B, with documented delta) or carry forward unchanged (Thesis A)? D5 blocks this page's voice/copy pass but not the structural changes.

breaksFromV1: author block above the fold consistency
single next-step at footer (not three competing)

currentState.summary: v1 post detail: title + date + tags + hero + MDX body + related-posts. Reflows cleanly on mobile.
currentState.annotation: v2 preserves the reading experience and adds cross-format framing (badge, byline, single next-step) for consistency across post / whitepaper / counterpoint surfaces.
